groups.name: PowerTech V6/V8 Family

groups.name: HEMI V8 Family

groups.name: EcoDiesel V6 Family

commonProblems.h2: Common Dodge Engine Problems — What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: Dodge engines in the UK are predominantly found in imported RAM trucks, performance models like Charger and Challenger, and legacy SUVs such as Nitro and Journey. The most prominent engine families driving replacement demand are the 2.8L CRD diesel, 3.7L PowerTech V6, and 5.7L HEMI V8, each with well-documented failure patterns among UK owners. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point — plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. The problem groups below are a decision tool: compare realistic repair costs against full replacement to make the right call for your vehicle and budget.

problems.h4: 2.8L CRD Turbo & Injection Failure – Why Dodge Nitro Diesel Engines Stall Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Dodge Nitro 2.8 CRD (2007–2012, engine code ENR/EDJ), Dodge Journey 2.8 CRD (2009–2014, ENR)
problems.rootCause: The Mercedes-sourced OM642-derived 2.8L CRD suffers from turbocharger vane sticking and high-pressure injector wear, exacerbated by short-trip UK driving that prevents full DPF regeneration cycles .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Replace turbo boost sensor, clean/replace actuator, reset ECU adaptations; diagnostic time included | Viable only if caught before turbo shaft damage; adds 15–25k miles if maintenance is strict

repairOptions.tier: | Intermediate – Injector Set Replacement + Turbo Service
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Remove and replace all four high-pressure injectors with calibrated units; turbo clean/service; fuel pressure test | Extends life 30–50k miles but does not address underlying oil-coking risk in the turbo; best for vehicles under £4k value

repairOptions.tier: | Full Replacement – Reconditioned 2.8 CRD Engine Supplied & Fitted
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Supply and fit of reconditioned ENR/EDJ engine with upgraded turbo oil feed line, new injectors, bench-tested; includes 12-month warranty | Most cost-effective long-term solution for Nitro/Journey owners; eliminates repeat failure risk and includes warranty protection

problems.recommendation: On a Dodge Nitro or Journey valued under £5,000, investing in injector replacement alone rarely pays off—the underlying turbo oil-coking issue often returns within 12–18 months. We recommend the full reconditioned engine route if the vehicle is otherwise sound; the upgraded oil feed and warranty provide genuine peace of mind for UK driving conditions.
problems.cta: → Compare reconditioned 2.8 CRD engine prices for your Nitro or Journey

problems.h4: 3.7L PowerTech V6 Timing Chain Failure – The Nitro & Dakota Weak Point
problems.affectedModels: Dodge Nitro 3.7L V6 (2007–2011, engine code EKG), Dodge Dakota 3.7L (2004–2010, EKG), Jeep Liberty 3.7L (2002–2012)
problems.rootCause: The 3.7L PowerTech uses a single-row timing chain with plastic guides that become brittle with age; tensioner wear allows chain slap, leading to guide fragmentation and potential valve interference .

repairOptions.tier: | Minor – Timing Chain Tensioner & Guide Refresh
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Replace primary tensioner and upper guides via valve cover access; oil change included | Only viable if caught before chain stretch causes cam timing error; adds ~20k miles if oil changes are strict

repairOptions.tier: | Full Replacement – Reconditioned 3.7L V6 Supplied & Fitted
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Supply and fit of reconditioned EKG engine with upgraded metal-guide timing set, new oil pump, bench-tested; 12-month warranty included | The only reliable solution once chain noise or misfire codes appear; eliminates repeat failure and protects vehicle value

problems.recommendation: If your 3.7L V6 is making a rattling noise on cold start, the chain has likely already damaged guides—attempting a tensioner-only fix risks catastrophic valve damage. For Nitro or Dakota owners, the reconditioned engine route is almost always the smarter financial decision, especially given the vehicle's residual value.
problems.cta: → Get quotes for rebuilt 3.7L EKG engines with upgraded timing components

problems.group: 3.6L Pentastar Valve Train & Oil System Issues
problems.h4: 3.6L Pentastar Valve Train & Oil System Issues – Ram, Charger & Journey
problems.affectedModels: Dodge Ram 1500 3.6L (2013–2023, ERB), Dodge Charger/Journey 3.6L (2011–2023, ERB), Chrysler 300 3.6L (2011–2023)
problems.rootCause: Early Pentastar engines (pre-2016) suffered from cylinder head casting porosity and cam phaser wear; oil dilution from short trips accelerates bearing wear, while carbon buildup on intake valves affects direct-injection variants .

repairOptions.tier: | Intermediate – Cylinder Head Repair + Phaser Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Remove and refurbish cylinder heads with updated valve seats; replace cam phasers and timing components; full gasket set | Viable for well-maintained engines with early warning signs (ticking, oil consumption); adds 40–60k miles if oil changes are frequent

repairOptions.tier: | Full Replacement – Reconditioned 3.6L Pentastar Supplied & Fitted
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Supply and fit of reconditioned ERB engine with updated cylinder heads, upgraded oil pump, bench-tested; includes 12-month warranty | Recommended for vehicles over £6k value or high-mileage use; eliminates repeat head/phaser failures and provides warranty security

problems.recommendation: If your Pentastar is consuming oil or making a ticking noise above 100k miles, head repair is a gamble—many engines re-fail within 18 months. For Ram 1500 or Charger owners planning to keep the vehicle, the reconditioned engine with updated components offers far better long-term value and peace of mind.
problems.cta: → Compare rebuilt 3.6L Pentastar engine prices for your Ram or Charger

problems.h4: 5.7L HEMI Lifter Collapse & Camshaft Wear – The Performance Model Risk
problems.affectedModels: Dodge Ram 1500 5.7L HEMI (2009–2023, EZH/EZL), Dodge Charger/Challenger 5.7L HEMI (2011–2023), Durango 5.7L HEMI
problems.rootCause: The HEMI's Multiple Displacement System (MDS) lifters can collapse due to oil aeration or delayed oil changes; collapsed lifters score the camshaft lobes, causing metallic ticking, loss of power, and eventual valve train failure .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Remove intake manifold and valve covers; replace all 16 lifters and camshaft; MDS solenoids refreshed; full oil system flush | Only viable if caught before metal debris circulates; adds ~30k miles but does not address underlying oiling design

repairOptions.tier: | Full Replacement – Reconditioned 5.7L HEMI Supplied & Fitted
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Supply and fit of reconditioned EZH/EZL engine with upgraded MDS lifters, revised oil pump, bench-tested; includes 12-month warranty | Strongly recommended for Charger/Challenger enthusiasts and high-mileage Ram owners; eliminates repeat lifter failure and protects performance investment

problems.recommendation: Once a HEMI develops a persistent lifter tick, metal debris is already circulating—attempting an in-car lifter swap risks damaging the new cam within months. For performance models or daily-driver Rams, the reconditioned HEMI with upgraded lifters is the only route that protects your investment and delivers reliable power.
problems.cta: → Compare rebuilt 5.7L HEMI engine prices for your Charger, Challenger or Ram

problems.h4: 3.0L EcoDiesel Oil Cooler & Bearing Failure – Ram 1500 Diesel
problems.affectedModels: Dodge Ram 1500 3.0L EcoDiesel (2014–2020, engine code EXN), Jeep Grand Cherokee 3.0L EcoDiesel (2014–2020)
problems.rootCause: The VM Motori-sourced 3.0L EcoDiesel suffers from oil cooler gasket failure, allowing coolant into the oil gallery; this dilutes oil film strength, accelerating connecting rod bearing wear and potentially causing catastrophic bottom-end failure .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Remove intake manifold; replace oil cooler assembly and gaskets; flush cooling and oil systems; pressure test | Only viable if caught before bearing wear begins (no knocking noise, normal oil pressure); adds ~25k miles with strict maintenance

repairOptions.tier: | Full Replacement – Reconditioned 3.0L EcoDiesel Supplied & Fitted
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Supply and fit of reconditioned EXN engine with upgraded oil cooler design, revised bearing clearances, bench-tested; includes 12-month warranty | Recommended once any bearing noise or oil/coolant mixing is detected; eliminates repeat failure risk and restores towing reliability

problems.recommendation: If your EcoDiesel shows any sign of oil/coolant mixing (milky oil, coolant loss) or bearing knock, do not attempt a cooler-only repair—the bearings are already compromised. For Ram 1500 owners who tow or cover high miles, the reconditioned engine with upgraded cooler is the only route that delivers long-term reliability.
problems.cta: → Get quotes for rebuilt 3.0L EcoDiesel engines with upgraded oil coolers

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Dodge
finalCta.paragraph: Every problem group above shares a common thread: once critical damage occurs, repair costs quickly approach or exceed the value of a UK-imported Dodge. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Dodge specialists who supply and fit reconditioned engines with upgraded components addressing the original failure point. All engines carry a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty, and our network covers London, Manchester and nationwide delivery. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Dodge Engine Replacement Prices Now
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Dodge model and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty — always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.h2: Dodge Engine Types – Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every Dodge engine type available through our UK network, with typical price ranges and recommendations based on your vehicle's age, condition and your budget.

types.title: Dodge Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: A stripped, cleaned and fully inspected engine where all worn components are replaced with OEM‑spec parts. Best for owners who want factory reliability without the factory price — ideal for Caliber, Journey and Nitro models where cost‑effective reliability is the priority.
types.priceRange: Typical price range: £1,200 – £4,500 (depending on engine code)

types.title: Dodge Rebuilt Engines
types.description: A complete rebuild with upgraded components addressing known failure points — such as reinforced timing chain kits for Pentastar V6 units and uprated injector seals for CRD diesels — for a longer service life than a standard replacement.

types.title: Used Dodge Engines
types.description: A tested, low‑mileage take‑out unit from a donor vehicle. The most affordable option when budget is the priority, with verified history and warranty available — suitable for older Nitro, Caliber or Journey models where upfront cost matters most.

types.title: Dodge Remanufactured Engines
types.description: A factory‑spec, as‑new engine: every surface is machined, every component is replaced, and the unit is built to meet exact OEM tolerances. The premium choice for performance models like Challenger, Charger or Ram 1500 HEMI owners wanting a zero‑mile engine.

types.title: Dodge Refurbished Engines
types.description: A lightly serviced, cleaned, and tested engine that works reliably but hasn't had a complete rebuild. Often a pragmatic, fast solution for older or budget‑sensitive models where a full recon isn't economical — ideal for pre‑2010 Dodge imports with lower market value.

types.title: Dodge Supply & Fit Engines
types.description: Your replacement engine delivered and professionally installed by a UK Dodge specialist. Includes removal of the old unit, fitting, fluids, and a workmanship warranty — perfect for owners who want a single, hassle‑free quote for the complete job.
types.priceRange: Typical added cost: £800 – £2,500 on top of engine price
types.cta: Find Dodge engine supply & fit deals

engineTypes.closing: All engine types include a minimum 12‑month warranty. Prices are typical UK market ranges and depend on exact engine code, supplier, and any additional fitting services. Use our free comparison tool to get a tailored quote for your Dodge model today.

engineSizes.h2: Dodge Engines by Size – Find the Right Displacement for Your Model
engineSizes.intro: Every Dodge engine size explained — with the models and engine codes that used each displacement — so you can quickly identify the correct replacement unit for your vehicle.

items.title: Dodge 2.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.2L CRD diesel was fitted primarily to the Dodge Caliber and select European-market Journey models, using the VM Motori-sourced ENE engine family. Production ran from 2007–2012, and while relatively uncommon in the UK, these units are sought after by Caliber owners facing injector or turbocharger failure — the most frequent catalysts for replacement demand
items.cta: Get quotes for Dodge 2.2L diesel engines

items.title: Dodge 2.8L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.8L CRD (ENR/EDJ) is the most widely encountered Dodge diesel in the UK, powering the Nitro and Journey between 2007–2014. Derived from the Mercedes OM642 architecture, this engine is prone to turbo vane sticking and oil-coking in the feed line — failure patterns that frequently drive owners toward reconditioned replacement units with upgraded components
items.cta: Get quotes for Dodge 2.8L diesel engines

items.title: Dodge 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.0L EcoDiesel (EXN) appears in UK-imported Ram 1500 and Grand Cherokee models from 2014–2020, offering strong torque for towing. Its most critical vulnerability is oil cooler gasket failure, which can allow coolant into the oil gallery and accelerate bearing wear — a failure mode that often necessitates full engine replacement rather than repair
items.cta: Get quotes for Dodge 3.0L diesel engines
commonFailurePoints: Oil cooler gasket failure leading to coolant/oil mixing

items.title: Dodge 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0L petrol engine (EDZ/EJD) was used in early Caliber and Avenger models sold in the UK between 2007–2012. While generally robust with proper maintenance, these units commonly suffer from ignition coil failure and oil consumption issues at higher mileages — problems that often prompt owners to compare reconditioned replacement options
items.cta: Compare Dodge 2.0L petrol engine prices
commonFailurePoints: oil consumption due to piston ring wear

items.title: Dodge 2.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.4L World Gas Engine (ED3/ED4) powered the Caliber, Journey, and Avenger across 2007–2014 UK sales. Known for variable valve timing solenoid issues and oil sludge buildup if service intervals are extended, this displacement frequently drives replacement searches when timing-related fault codes appear alongside performance loss
items.cta: Compare Dodge 2.4L petrol engine prices
commonFailurePoints: oil sludge buildup from extended service intervals

items.title: Dodge 3.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.6L Pentastar V6 (ERB/ERG) is Dodge's most ubiquitous petrol engine in the UK, found in Ram 1500, Charger, Challenger, Journey and Durango from 2011–present. Early production units (pre-2016) are susceptible to cylinder head casting porosity and cam phaser wear — failure patterns that commonly trigger replacement enquiries among UK owners of imported models
items.cta: Compare Dodge 3.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Dodge 3.7L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.7L PowerTech V6 (EKG) was fitted to the Nitro and Dakota models sold in the UK between 2004–2011. Its single-row timing chain with plastic guides is the dominant failure point — guide fragmentation can lead to chain jump and valve interference, making this displacement a frequent candidate for full reconditioned replacement rather than in-car repair
items.cta: Compare Dodge 3.7L petrol engine prices
commonFailurePoints: tensioner wear leading to chain jump

items.title: Dodge 5.7L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 5.7L HEMI V8 (EZH/EZL/EZB) powers the Ram 1500, Charger, Challenger and Durango in the UK import market from 2009–present. Its Multiple Displacement System (MDS) lifters are prone to collapse due to oil aeration or delayed oil changes, scoring camshaft lobes and triggering the metallic ticking that often precedes full engine replacement enquiries among performance and towing-focused owners
items.cta: Compare Dodge 5.7L petrol engine prices

engineSizes.closing: Not sure which engine size your Dodge needs? Enter your registration number and we'll match you to the exact engine code and displacement — instantly. All engines supplied with a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Dodge specialists.

fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Dodge engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known brand‑wide issues, and UK specialist supply – from the most popular diesel workhorses to the latest electrified powertrains.

items.title: Dodge Diesel Engines
items.description: Dodge diesel engines cover many of the brand's highest-mileage UK models. Replacement demand is usually driven by timing system wear, injector and turbo faults, plus EGR or DPF-related damage once repeat short-journey use starts affecting reliability.
knownFor: High-pressure injector wear
turbocharger actuator failure
Turbocharger vane sticking
high-pressure injector wear
importantNotes: Short-journey use increases DPF, EGR, and oil-dilution risk on many diesel engines.
A documented service history matters more on diesel replacements because injector, turbo, and timing faults often build gradually.
items.cta: Compare Dodge diesel engine prices

items.title: Dodge Petrol Engines
items.description: Dodge petrol engines span everything from core daily-driver models to higher-output performance variants. Most replacement enquiries start with timing chain noise, oil consumption, cooling-system failure, or internal wear that makes a warranted replacement more sensible than repeated repairs.
knownFor: Ignition coil failure
oil consumption due to piston ring wear
VVT solenoid failure
oil sludge buildup from extended service intervals
importantNotes: Cold-start rattles, overheating history, and oil use are the biggest warning signs before a petrol engine replacement becomes necessary.
Direct-injection petrol engines often need specialist attention for carbon build-up alongside the main mechanical repair.
items.cta: Compare Dodge petrol engine prices

fuelTypes.closing: Not sure which fuel type your Dodge has? Enter your registration number above to instantly identify your model, engine code, fuel system and compatible replacement options – all backed by a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Dodge specialists.

engineCodeDirectory.h2: Dodge Engine Codes – Most Replaced Engines (2008–2025)
engineCodeDirectory.intro: Every major Dodge engine code from the last two decades, grouped by family, with compatible models and typical reasons why owners seek replacement. Select your engine to compare rebuilt prices and get quotes from UK Dodge specialists.

entries.title: ED3 2.0L Diesel
entries.description: Originally fitted to the Dodge Caliber and Avenger (PM/JS, 2007–2012), this CRD unit is frequently replaced due to injector seal leaks and turbo actuator fatigue. Diesel ingress into the sump causes rapid oil dilution and bearing wear, making partial repairs economically unviable for many owners. Quality UK-rebuilt units incorporate reinforced injector seats, revised turbo actuators, and a complete timing chain kit to eliminate repeat failures.
entries.cta: Get quotes for ED3 Dodge engine replacement

entries.title: ED4 2.0L Diesel
entries.description: The high-output variant found in the Avenger and Caliber (2007–2012) suffers primarily from severe EGR valve clogging and high-pressure fuel pump wear. When left unaddressed, restricted exhaust flow and fuel pressure drops lead to misfires and eventual piston ring scoring. Specialist rebuilt engines address these weaknesses with upgraded EGR coolers, precision-machined lifters, and a full set of reinforced head bolts.
entries.cta: Compare ED4 Dodge engine prices

entries.title: ENE 2.2L Diesel
entries.description: Powering the Dodge Journey and later Caliber diesels (2011–2014), this unit is notorious for cam follower collapse and premature oil pump wear. Loss of oil pressure at the top end quickly destroys valve train components, leaving the block intact but irreparable without major intervention. Reconditioned replacements feature upgraded cam followers, high-volume oil pumps, and revised crankshaft bearings for extended durability.
entries.cta: Find rebuilt ENE Dodge engines

entries.title: EDJ 2.2L Diesel
entries.description: Common in the Avenger and Journey (2011–2014), this displacement is closely related to the ENE but experiences accelerated timing chain stretch under heavy UK motorway loads. Combined with frequent DPF blockages causing excessive backpressure, the engine often suffers catastrophic timing jumps. Our network supplies rebuilt units fitted with heavy-duty timing chains, reinforced tensioners, and updated DPF-compatible exhaust manifolds.
entries.cta: Request EDJ Dodge engine supply quotes

entries.title: EXB 2.8L Diesel
entries.description: The larger CRD, widely used in the Nitro and Journey (2007–2012), is most commonly replaced following turbo bearing seizure and main bearing starvation. Oil dilution from faulty injectors and prolonged cold-start cycles strips protective films, leading to spun bearings. Professionally rebuilt EXB engines include forged crank bearings, uprated turbochargers, and reinforced oil pump pickup screens to prevent recurrence.
entries.cta: Compare EXB 2.8L Dodge engine costs

entries.title: EDV 1.8L Petrol
entries.description: A compact petrol found in the Caliber (2007–2012), this World Gas engine frequently fails due to hydraulic timing chain tensioner collapse and excessive oil consumption. Once the tensioner bottoms out, chain slap damages guides and can jump timing entirely, bending valves. Replacement units resolve this with upgraded hydraulic tensioners, reinforced piston rings, and new valve stem seals for reliable long-term operation.
entries.cta: Get quotes for EDV Dodge petrol engine

entries.title: EDZ 2.4L Petrol
entries.description: Fitted across the Caliber, Avenger, and Journey (2007–2016), this petrol is prone to oil pump pickup screen clogging and subsequent bearing starvation. Sludge buildup restricts flow during cold starts, rapidly accelerating crankshaft and conrod bearing wear. Rebuilt engines feature a redesigned high-flow oil pump assembly, reinforced main bearings, and updated timing guides to restore factory reliability.
entries.cta: Compare EDZ 2.4L Dodge engine prices

entries.title: EDK 2.4L Petrol
entries.description: Serving the Grand Caravan and Journey (2008–2016), this petrol variant suffers from exhaust manifold stud breakage and head gasket degradation. Thermal cycling cracks the factory manifold, allowing hot gases to burn through gaskets and compromise cylinder sealing. Specialist replacements include multi-layer steel head gaskets, reinforced manifold studs, and precision-machined cylinder heads for leak-free performance.
entries.cta: Find rebuilt EDK Dodge engines

entries.title: EKG 3.7L V6 Petrol
entries.description: The PowerTech V6 used in the Nitro and Dakota (2002–2012) is heavily targeted for replacement due to timing chain guide disintegration and ignition coil failure. Nylon guides shatter over time, sending debris through the timing case and jamming the chain, while weak coils cause destructive misfires. Reconditioned units feature composite timing guides, upgraded ignition coils, and reinforced valve springs to withstand UK mileage demands.
entries.cta: Get quotes for EKG V6 Dodge engine replacement

entries.title: EPH 4.7L V8 Petrol
entries.description: Powering the Durango and Dakota (2002–2012), this V8 frequently fails when the oil pump pickup screen clogs and the timing chain jumps. The combination causes catastrophic bearing starvation and valve-to-piston contact, often rendering repair uneconomical. UK-rebuilt replacements install a triple-row timing chain kit, high-volume oil pumps, and upgraded crank dampeners for robust daily driving.
entries.cta: Compare EPH 4.7L Dodge engine prices

entries.title: ERB 3.6L V6 Petrol
entries.description: The base Pentastar in the Charger and Challenger (2011–Present) is widely replaced following early-production cylinder head porosity and oil cooler gasket failure. Coolant leaks into the oil system, emulsifying lubrication and rapidly destroying cam bearings. Quality rebuilt engines incorporate revised cylinder head castings, braided oil cooler lines, and upgraded head bolts to permanently seal these weak points.
entries.cta: Find rebuilt ERB Pentastar engines

entries.title: ERC 3.6L V6 Petrol
entries.description: Fitted to the Ram 1500 and Durango (2013–Present), this commercial-duty variant suffers from rocker arm trunnion failure and thermostat housing cracks. Broken rocker arms pierce valve covers, while housing fractures lead to sudden coolant loss and overheating. Specialist rebuilds include forged rocker arms, updated thermostat housings, and precision-machined decks for heavy-duty reliability.
entries.cta: Request ERC 3.6L Dodge engine quotes

entries.title: ERG 3.6L V6 Petrol
entries.description: Common in the Journey and Grand Caravan (2011–2018), this family-oriented version is often replaced after water pump bearing failure and timing tensioner wear. Pump failure causes rapid overheating, while a worn tensioner leads to chain stretch and cam timing drift. Reconditioned units come equipped with high-capacity water pumps, reinforced tensioner assemblies, and complete serpentine drive kits.
entries.cta: Compare ERG Dodge engine replacement costs

entries.title: EZB 5.7L V8 Petrol
entries.description: The iconic HEMI V8 (Ram, Charger, Challenger, 2003–Present) is most frequently replaced due to VVT lifter collapse and subsequent camshaft lobe wear. Oil pressure fluctuations or sludge cause lifters to flatten, grinding down the cam lobe and sending metal debris through the oil galley. Premium rebuilt units feature uprated VVT lifters, hardened camshafts, and reinforced valve springs to eliminate the infamous HEMI tick.
entries.cta: Get quotes for EZB 5.7L HEMI Dodge engine

entries.title: EZH 5.7L V8 Petrol
entries.description: A high-compression HEMI used in Charger R/T, Challenger, and Durango (2009–Present) suffers from exhaust manifold bolt breakage and persistent lifter tick. Broken studs allow exhaust leaks that warp heads, while worn lifters degrade performance and oil pressure. Specialist replacements include stainless steel manifold studs, redesigned exhaust gaskets, and updated lifter guides for sustained power delivery.
entries.cta: Compare EZH HEMI Dodge engine prices

entries.title: ESF 6.4L V8 Petrol
entries.description: The 6.4L 392 HEMI powering Challenger/Charger SRT models (2011–Present) is often replaced following piston ring land failure under sustained high-RPM driving and oil pressure fluctuations. Factory rings flex under extreme cylinder pressure, allowing blow-by that rapidly degrades crankcase ventilation. Rebuilt SRT engines feature forged pistons, revised oil pressure regulators, and uprated crank bearings for track-ready durability.
entries.cta: Find rebuilt ESF 6.4L Dodge engines

entries.title: ECO3 3.0L V6 Diesel
entries.description: The EcoDiesel V6 (Ram 1500, Durango, 2014–Present) faces high replacement demand due to EGR cooler cracking and turbo actuator failure. Thermal cycling fractures the cooler, allowing coolant into the intake and potentially causing hydro-lock, while actuators seize under soot buildup. Quality UK-rebuilt units install billet EGR coolers, reinforced turbo actuators, and upgraded intercooler piping for reliable towing performance.
entries.cta: Compare ECO3 EcoDiesel Dodge engine costs

entries.title: HEL 6.2L V8 Petrol
entries.description: The supercharged 6.2L Hellcat (Challenger/Charger, 2015–Present) requires specialist replacement when high-stress valvetrain fatigue and supercharger belt slippage cause catastrophic internal damage. Extreme cylinder pressures accelerate cam lobe wear and bearing fatigue, while belt issues trigger timing desynchronisation. Expert rebuilt engines include performance-grade camshafts, uprated supercharger pulleys, and reinforced connecting rods for maximum output.
entries.cta: Request HEL Hellcat Dodge engine quotes

entries.title: EWA 8.4L V10 Petrol
entries.description: The legendary V10 powering the Viper SRT-10 and ACR (2008–2017) is replaced due to oil pump starvation during high-G cornering and valve spring fatigue. Factory wet sump systems struggle under track loads, while weak springs lead to valve float and piston contact. Specialist V10 rebuilds incorporate dry-sump oil system retrofit components, dual valve springs, and titanium retainers for uncompromised track performance.
entries.cta: Get specialist quotes for EWA Viper V10 engine

entries.title: EXD 3.6L V6 Petrol
entries.description: The updated Pentastar (Charger/Challenger, 2014–Present) benefits from early corrections but still fails due to water pump seal leaks and variable valve timing solenoid sticking. Coolant ingress and VVT hesitation compromise cam timing, leading to rough running and eventual head gasket stress. Rebuilt units feature upgraded water pump seals, revised VVT solenoids, and reinforced cylinder liners for extended service intervals.
entries.cta: Compare EXD 3.6L Dodge engine prices

engineCodeDirectory.closing: Can’t find your engine code listed above? Use our full Dodge engine code directory below — every code from the last 30 years — or enter your registration number for an instant match to the correct replacement engine with warranty.

directory.h3: Full Dodge Engine Code Directory (All Years)
directory.label: “View all Dodge engine codes (85+ codes, 1990–2025)”
directory.intro: A comprehensive alphabetical listing of every Dodge engine code from the last three decades. Use the search above or enter your registration for an instant match to your specific engine. All codes link to a quote request.

engineYears.h2: Dodge Engines by Year – What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers every major Dodge engine change across the full UK-available model range from the early 2000s to the present day, organised by year so owners can identify their exact engine family, understand what was fitted and when, and compare reconditioned or replacement engine prices from verified UK specialists.

years.preview: Gen III HEMI 5.7L Arrives: The Modern Muscle Era Begins Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The 2003 model year marked the reintroduction of the legendary HEMI nameplate on the new Gen III 5.7L V8 - a defining moment in Dodge's modern history that would power the Challenger, Charger, Durango, and Ram for the next two decades.
keyChanges: Gen III 5.7L HEMI (EZB, EZH) debuts in Dodge Ram 1500 pickup for 2003 - replaces aging Magnum V8 pushrod family
5.7L HEMI introduced to Dodge Durango (2004) and Dodge Magnum/Charger/Challenger (2005–2006 rollout)
Cummins 5.9L 12-valve/24-valve ISB continues in Ram 2500/3500 heavy-duty trucks as the benchmark diesel
3.7L V6 Magnum (EKG) and 4.7L V8 PowerTech (EKD) remain available on Ram 1500 and Durango as entry powerplants
No cylinder deactivation (MDS) on initial Gen III HEMI - added later in revised units
mainEngines: EZB / EZH - 5.7L V8 HEMI petrol (Dodge Ram 1500, Durango, Magnum)
EKG - 3.7L V6 Magnum petrol (Dodge Ram 1500, Durango)
EKD / EVA - 4.7L V8 PowerTech petrol (Dodge Ram 1500, Durango, Dakota)
ISB 5.9L 24v - Cummins turbo diesel (Dodge Ram 2500/3500)
knownFor: Gen III 5.7L HEMI immediately earns a strong reputation for torque delivery and durability - commonly regarded as one of the most robust American V8s of the modern era
Cummins 5.9L ISB diesel in Ram HD trucks is arguably the most desirable engine in the Dodge truck lineage - extremely high replacement demand and a well-developed UK specialist market
4.7L PowerTech V8 is a functional but uninspiring unit; valve seat recession at high mileage and coolant passage corrosion are the most common specialist complaints
Early Gen III HEMI without MDS has simpler valvetrain architecture - these pre-MDS units are preferred by performance specialists for longevity
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Dodge 5.7 HEMI engine replacement - Dodge Ram 1500 HEMI engine for sale UK - Cummins 5.9 ISB engine reconditioned - Dodge Durango 5.7L engine replacement
years.cta: Compare quotes for 2003–2005 Dodge HEMI and Cummins replacement engines from UK specialists

years.preview: MDS Cylinder Deactivation & 6.1L SRT HEMI Introduction Technology Introduction Performance Landmark
years.description: Dodge introduced Multi-Displacement System (MDS) cylinder deactivation on the 5.7L HEMI across the Charger, Challenger, and Durango - and simultaneously launched the high-revving 6.1L SRT HEMI as the brand's new performance flagship.
keyChanges: MDS (Multi-Displacement System) cylinder deactivation added to 5.7L HEMI (EZD, EZE codes) - shuts down 4 cylinders at light load for improved fuel economy
6.1L SRT HEMI (ESF) debuts on Dodge Charger SRT8 and Challenger SRT8 - 425hp naturally aspirated V8 with forged internals
Second-generation Dodge Charger (LX) and Dodge Challenger (LC) launched in the UK as imported grey/official performance models
3rd-gen Cummins 6.7L inline-six diesel (ISB 6.7L) replaces outgoing 5.9L in Ram HD trucks from 2007 - cleaner emissions, larger displacement
4.7L V8 updated (EVE) with improved fuel management for Ram 1500
mainEngines: EZD / EZE - 5.7L V8 HEMI with MDS petrol (Dodge Charger, Challenger, Durango, Ram 1500)
ESF - 6.1L V8 SRT HEMI petrol (Dodge Charger SRT8, Challenger SRT8)
ISB 6.7L - Cummins turbo diesel (Dodge Ram 2500/3500 HD)
EVE - 4.7L V8 petrol updated (Dodge Ram 1500, Dakota)
knownFor: MDS-equipped 5.7L HEMI (EZD) is the most common engine replacement engine in the Dodge catalogue - MDS lifter collapse and cam lobe wear between 80,000–120,000 miles are the single biggest driver of replacement demand in the UK
6.1L SRT HEMI is a robust, high-compression naturally aspirated unit - fewer reliability concerns than MDS-equipped brothers, though it demands quality synthetic oil and strict service intervals
Cummins 6.7L ISB introduces diesel particulate filter for first time - DPF issues on short-cycle UK use become a recurring workshop concern
MDS deactivation solenoid failure is an early-warning sign before full lifter failure - UK specialists universally advise addressing at first tick, not after noise develops
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Dodge 5.7 HEMI MDS lifter failure engine replacement - Dodge Charger SRT8 6.1 HEMI engine for sale - Dodge Ram 5.7 HEMI replacement UK - Cummins 6.7 ISB reconditioned engine - Dodge Challenger HEMI engine replacement
years.cta: Find replacement 2006–2008 Dodge 5.7 HEMI and 6.1 SRT engines with UK warranty

years.preview: Revised 5.7L HEMI with VVT & Challenger Relaunch Major Reliability Revision
years.description: For the 2009 model year, Chrysler revised the 5.7L HEMI with Variable Valve Timing (VVT) - improving both low-end torque and fuel economy - while the Dodge Challenger (LC platform) began its full US production run, generating significant UK grey-import demand.
keyChanges: 5.7L HEMI updated with Variable Valve Timing (VVT) for 2009 - new codes EZH (revised), improved torque curve and slightly better economy
Revised MDS system calibration improves transition smoothness but fundamental lifter-wear risk remains
Dodge Challenger R/T and SRT8 enter full production - 5.7L and 6.1L HEMI units confirmed across trim range
6.4L Apache V8 HEMI development begins (previewed in Ram SRT-10 / pre-production engineering)
3.5L High-Output V6 (EGG, EGH) fitted to base Dodge Charger and Avenger in US market - not widely imported to UK but relevant to parallel-import buyers
mainEngines: EZH (revised VVT) - 5.7L V8 HEMI VVT petrol (Dodge Ram 1500, Challenger R/T, Charger R/T)
ESF - 6.1L V8 SRT HEMI petrol (Dodge Charger SRT8, Challenger SRT8 final year)
ISB 6.7L - Cummins turbo diesel (Dodge Ram 2500/3500)
EGG / EGH - 3.5L V6 petrol (Dodge Charger base, Avenger - US spec / grey import)
knownFor: VVT revision on 5.7L HEMI is a genuine improvement - idle refinement and off-idle torque notably better than pre-2009 units; VVT phaser wear at high mileage is a new monitoring point
Challenger R/T with 5.7L HEMI becomes the most popular UK grey-import Dodge muscle car of the modern era - strong community and aftermarket support in Britain
6.1L SRT HEMI nears the end of its production run before 6.4L takes over for 2011 - last-of-line units in excellent condition are highly sought after by UK enthusiasts
Cummins 6.7L diesel increasingly affected by EGR cooler failures on UK-operated trucks - a recurring problem at 100,000 miles that specialists widely recognise
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Dodge Challenger 5.7 HEMI engine replacement UK - Dodge Charger 6.1 SRT HEMI for sale - Dodge Ram 1500 VVT HEMI reconditioned - Cummins 6.7 EGR failure engine replacement
years.cta: Get quotes for 2009–2010 Dodge VVT HEMI and SRT replacement engines

years.preview: 3.6L Pentastar V6, 6.4L 392 HEMI & New Platform Era Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: Two landmark engine introductions define this period: the all-new 3.6L Pentastar V6 (ERB, ERH) replacing all previous V6 units across the Challenger, Charger, and Durango, and the 6.4L 392 HEMI (ESA) taking over from the outgoing 6.1L as the brand's naturally aspirated performance crown.
keyChanges: 3.6L Pentastar V6 (ERB) introduced across Dodge Challenger, Charger, Durango, Journey, Avenger from 2011 - replaces 2.7L, 3.5L, and 4.0L V6 family
6.4L 392 HEMI (ESA, ESB) debuts on Challenger SRT8 and Charger SRT8 - 470hp naturally aspirated benchmark
5.7L HEMI (EZH) continues across R/T and GT trim levels with revised MDS calibration
Variable Valve Timing (VVT) now standard on Pentastar 3.6L from launch - strong fuel economy across the model range
Euro 5 equivalent emissions standard met by US PZEV (Partial Zero Emissions Vehicle) variants where applicable
mainEngines: ERB / ERH - 3.6L V6 Pentastar petrol (Dodge Challenger, Charger, Durango, Journey)
ESA / ESB - 6.4L V8 392 HEMI petrol (Dodge Challenger SRT8, Charger SRT8)
EZH - 5.7L V8 HEMI VVT MDS petrol (Dodge Challenger R/T, Charger R/T, Durango)
ISB 6.7L - Cummins diesel (Dodge Ram 2500/3500)
knownFor: 3.6L Pentastar is a broadly capable V6 but early units (2011–2013 build) suffer from rocker arm/cam follower failure - ticking noise followed by cylinder head damage is the primary UK workshop complaint
6.4L 392 HEMI earns near-universal praise among UK Dodge enthusiasts - forged crankshaft, high-compression design, and MDS (when fitted: ESA/ESB) that is generally more tolerant than 5.7L equivalents
5.7L MDS lifter failures remain the single most-searched Dodge engine problem in the UK throughout this period - replacement demand peaks during 2011–2014 era vehicles hitting the 80,000-mile mark
Pentastar 3.6L suffers from water pump/coolant system failures at higher mileage; coolant contamination of oil via internal passages is an escalating failure mode if ignored
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Dodge 3.6 Pentastar engine replacement - Dodge Challenger 6.4 HEMI engine for sale UK - Dodge 5.7 HEMI lifter failure engine replacement - Dodge Durango 3.6 engine reconditioned - Dodge Charger SRT8 392 engine UK
years.cta: Compare 2011–2014 Dodge Pentastar V6 and 392 HEMI replacement engine prices

years.preview: 6.2L Hellcat Supercharged HEMI: 707hp Era Begins Performance Landmark
years.description: The arrival of the supercharged 6.2L Hellcat HEMI in 2015 redefined what a production muscle car engine could be - 707 horsepower from a factory-built V8 supercharged unit across the Challenger and Charger SRT Hellcat, instantly becoming the most talked-about American engine of its generation.
keyChanges: 6.2L Supercharged V8 HEMI Hellcat (EJL) debuts in 2015 Challenger and Charger SRT Hellcat - 707hp, IHI twin-screw 2.4L supercharger
6.2L Demon variant (EJR) introduced for 2018 Challenger SRT Demon - 808hp on 91-octane, 840hp on 100-octane; production limited to 3,300 units globally
Pentastar 3.6L (ERH) updated with revised rocker arm design to address cam follower failures from early production
5.7L HEMI continues as the volume R/T engine - MDS-related lifter failure replacement demand at its highest peak across UK specialist workshops
Durango SRT first offered with 6.4L 392 HEMI (2018) - performance SUV segment in the UK grey-import market grows
mainEngines: EJL - 6.2L V8 Supercharged HEMI Hellcat petrol (Dodge Challenger SRT Hellcat, Charger SRT Hellcat)
EJR - 6.2L V8 Supercharged HEMI Demon petrol (Dodge Challenger SRT Demon 2018)
ESA / ESG - 6.4L V8 392 HEMI petrol (Dodge Challenger SRT 392, Charger SRT 392, Durango SRT)
EZH - 5.7L V8 HEMI VVT MDS petrol (Dodge Challenger R/T, Charger R/T, Durango GT)
ERH - 3.6L V6 Pentastar petrol revised (Dodge Challenger SXT, Charger SXT, Durango)
knownFor: Hellcat 6.2L is widely regarded by UK performance specialists as one of the most reliable high-output engines on the market when properly maintained - no MDS, forged internals, and overbuilt construction for the power level

Supercharger snout seal and bearing wear is the primary maintenance concern on high-mileage or heavily driven Hellcat units - rebuild cost is significant but specialist availability in the UK is growing
6.2L Demon is among the most collectible modern American engines; UK imports are predominantly privately kept and low-mileage, meaning replacement demand is rare but parts availability is limited
5.7L HEMI replacement enquiries from 2015–2017-plate Challengers and Chargers in the UK represent the highest single-model search volume in the Dodge replacement engine category
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Dodge Hellcat 6.2 engine replacement UK - Dodge Challenger SRT 392 engine for sale - Dodge 5.7 HEMI reconditioned engine - Dodge Charger Hellcat engine rebuild - Dodge Durango SRT 6.4 engine UK
years.cta: Find 2015–2018 Dodge Hellcat and 392 HEMI replacement engines from UK performance specialists

years.preview: Redeye 797hp, Durango Hellcat & Widebody Era Performance Landmark
years.description: Dodge escalated its supercharged HEMI performance programme further with the 797hp Challenger Hellcat Redeye (2019) and the limited-run Durango SRT Hellcat (2021) - making this the peak of naturally aspirated and forced-induction HEMI development before the electrification transition.
keyChanges: 6.2L Hellcat Redeye (EJL upgraded) debuts in 2019 Challenger - 797hp using larger 2.7L supercharger vs base Hellcat's 2.4L unit
Charger SRT Hellcat Redeye follows for 2021 model year - widebody standard
Durango SRT Hellcat (2021) becomes the world's most powerful production SUV at launch - limited US production, very rare UK import
6.4L 392 HEMI continues across Challenger SRT 392 Widebody and Charger Scat Pack Widebody
3.6L Pentastar updated for improved emissions and reliability - revised valve train addresses earlier cam follower concerns more comprehensively
mainEngines: EJL (Redeye tune) - 6.2L V8 Supercharged HEMI Redeye 797hp (Dodge Challenger Hellcat Redeye, Charger Hellcat Redeye)
EJL - 6.2L V8 Supercharged HEMI standard 717hp (Dodge Challenger/Charger SRT Hellcat base)
ESG / ESH - 6.4L V8 392 HEMI MDS petrol (Dodge Challenger Scat Pack, Charger Scat Pack, Durango SRT)
EZH - 5.7L V8 HEMI VVT MDS petrol (Dodge Challenger R/T, Charger R/T, Durango GT/R/T)
ERH - 3.6L V6 Pentastar petrol (Dodge Challenger SXT, Charger SXT, Durango)
popularModels: Dodge Challenger SRT Hellcat Redeye Widebody
knownFor: Redeye 2.7L supercharger unit generates meaningfully higher underbonnet heat than base Hellcat - intercooler condition and supercharger coolant circuit are priority inspection points on used UK imports
6.4L 392 Scat Pack widebody models are the most imported non-Hellcat Dodge performance cars in the UK at this time - strong aftermarket, growing specialist network
Durango SRT Hellcat is a true collector's piece in the UK - US-spec only, left-hand drive, but demand from UK enthusiasts means full engine replacement support is a specialist enquiry
MDS lifter failure on 5.7L R/T models continues to be the highest-volume Dodge engine replacement category in the UK - typically presenting between 70,000–100,000 miles
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Dodge Challenger Hellcat Redeye engine for sale UK - Dodge 5.7 HEMI R/T engine replacement - Dodge Charger Scat Pack 6.4 engine reconditioned - Dodge Durango engine replacement - Dodge Challenger 5.7 HEMI lifter failure repair
years.cta: Get quotes for 2019–2021 Dodge Redeye, Scat Pack, and R/T replacement engines

years.preview: Final HEMI Years: Last Chance Edition, Jailbreak & Super Stock Performance Landmark
years.description: Dodge publicly announced the end of the HEMI V8 era across the Challenger and Charger nameplates, creating unprecedented demand for final-year cars and triggering a wave of commemorative editions - making 2022–2023 the last model years for traditional HEMI-powered muscle car production.
keyChanges: Dodge announces end of Challenger and Charger HEMI production - final 2023 "Last Call" editions announced covering seven special models
807hp Challenger SRT Jailbreak and Super Stock (6.2L EJL) introduced as final ultra-high-output variants
Challenger SRT Demon 170 (2023) replaces original Demon - 1,025hp on E85, the most powerful series-production car at time of announcement
5.7L HEMI production in Challenger and Charger confirmed to end with 2023 model year
3.6L Pentastar continues in Durango after Challenger/Charger line ends
mainEngines: EJL (Demon 170 / Jailbreak) - 6.2L V8 Supercharged HEMI up to 1,025hp (Challenger Demon 170, Jailbreak, Super Stock)
EJL - 6.2L V8 Supercharged HEMI 717–797hp (Challenger/Charger Hellcat, Hellcat Redeye)
ESG / ESH - 6.4L V8 392 HEMI MDS (Challenger Scat Pack, Charger Scat Pack)
EZH - 5.7L V8 HEMI MDS final production (Challenger R/T, Charger R/T)
ERH - 3.6L V6 Pentastar (Dodge Durango, Challenger SXT - final year)
popularModels: Dodge Challenger R/T Scat Pack (Last Call editions)
knownFor: Demon 170's 1,025hp on E85 makes it the most powerful series-production car ever built at the time - an extraordinary engineering statement at the close of the HEMI era
Final-year Last Call Challengers are increasingly collector's cars in the UK grey-import market - pristine-condition examples are purchased specifically for preservation
MDS lifter failure on late 5.7L R/T variants remains the dominant replacement engine enquiry for pre-owned examples entering the used market post-production
6.4L Scat Pack remains the most pragmatic high-performance Dodge engine in the UK - easier supercharger maintenance cost versus Hellcat, strong tuning response, and growing specialist knowledge base
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Dodge Challenger 5.7 HEMI final year engine replacement - Dodge Charger Scat Pack 6.4 engine UK - Dodge Challenger Hellcat engine reconditioned - Dodge 5.7 HEMI last call engine for sale UK
years.cta: Browse final-year 2022–2023 Dodge HEMI replacement engines - Hellcat, 392, and R/T units available

years.preview: Dodge Charger Daytona EV & Hurricane Six-Cylinder Transition Technology Introduction Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: Dodge pivots dramatically from the HEMI V8 legacy - the new Charger Daytona launches as the brand's first EV muscle car, while the 3.0L Hurricane twin-turbo inline-six (GME-T6) arrives as the combustion successor for those requiring an ICE-powered Charger, marking the most transformational engine transition in the brand's history.
keyChanges: New Dodge Charger Daytona (2024) launches as a fully electric muscle car - dual electric motors, no combustion engine
3.0L Hurricane twin-turbo inline-six (GME-T6) introduced in Charger Daytona Sixpack H.O. variant - replaces HEMI V8 for combustion buyers
Hurricane Standard Output (SO) produces 420hp; High Output (HO) delivers 550hp - both targeting HEMI replacement across future Dodge and Ram product
Cummins 6.7L ISB continues in Ram 2500/3500 HD - no immediate replacement announced
Dodge Durango remains the last HEMI-powered Dodge in production with 5.7L V8 as of 2025–2026
mainEngines: GME-T6 SO - 3.0L Twin-Turbo I6 Hurricane standard output 420hp (Dodge Charger Daytona Sixpack)
GME-T6 HO - 3.0L Twin-Turbo I6 Hurricane high output 550hp (Dodge Charger Daytona Sixpack H.O.)
BEV - Dual electric motors (Dodge Charger Daytona EV)
EZH - 5.7L V8 HEMI VVT MDS (Dodge Durango GT - continuing production)
ISB 6.7L - Cummins diesel (Dodge Ram 2500/3500 HD - continuing)
popularModels: Dodge Durango GT / R/T (final HEMI application)
knownFor: Hurricane GME-T6 is purpose-designed for longitudinal mounting and future electrification integration - twin-turbo architecture promises strong long-term replacement and rebuild ecosystem as volumes grow
Charger EV receives a mixed reception from the traditional UK Dodge grey-import community - performance figures are impressive but the absence of HEMI soundtrack is a divisive departure
Durango with 5.7L HEMI remains the last point of entry for traditional Dodge V8 buyers - replacement engine demand for Durango 5.7L is expected to increase as production winds down
Hurricane twin-turbo is too new for long-term replacement data - UK specialists advise monitoring turbocharged oil feed lines and intercooler integrity as primary early maintenance priorities
years.cta: Enquire about current-generation Dodge Hurricane and final-HEMI Durango replacement engine availability from UK specialists

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Dodge was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12-month warranty from UK Dodge specialists.

faq.h2: Dodge Engine Replacement – Frequently Asked Questions
faq.intro: ** Quick, honest answers to the most common questions Dodge owners ask before comparing engine replacement prices and finding a UK specialist.

items.question: How much does a Dodge engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned Dodge engine typically costs £2,200–£5,800 depending on size, with 4‑cylinder units like the 2.4L ED3 at the lower end and 5.7L HEMI V8s at the premium. Supply‑and‑fit labour adds £600–£1,800, while main dealers often charge 40–70% more. For budget options, used Dodge engines range £800–£2,500 but carry shorter warranties. Always compare multiple quotes to find the best value for your specific engine code.
items.cta: Compare Dodge engine replacement prices now

items.question: What affects the cost of a Dodge engine replacement?
items.answer: Four key factors drive pricing: engine displacement (3.6L Pentastar vs 5.7L HEMI), condition (used vs reconditioned), vehicle complexity (Ram 1500 takes longer than Caliber), and supply‑only vs supply‑and‑fit service. Location matters too—London fitting costs can be 20–30% higher than regional specialists. Our comparison tool provides transparent, itemised quotes so you see exactly what you're paying for before booking.
items.cta: Get quotes from UK Dodge engine specialists

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my Dodge?
items.answer: In most cases yes, especially if the chassis, gearbox and interior are solid. A rebuilt Dodge engine with upgraded components (like revised Pentastar cylinder heads or HEMI MDS lifters) often outlasts the original unit. The alternative—buying a replacement vehicle—typically costs far more than a £2,200–£5,800 reconditioned engine. If your Dodge holds value or has sentimental worth, replacement is almost always the smarter financial decision.
items.cta: Find reconditioned Dodge engines with comprehensive warranty cover

items.question: What's included in a Dodge engine supply and fit service?
items.answer: Supply‑and‑fit covers sourcing the correct engine code (e.g., ERB Pentastar, EZH HEMI), removing the old unit, installing the replacement with new gaskets and fluids, replacing ancillary filters, and a post‑fit test‑drive. Disposal of the old engine and a workmanship warranty are typically included. Labour for fitting ranges £600–£1,800 depending on model complexity—always confirm whether VAT and delivery are included in your quote.
items.cta: Compare Dodge engine supply and fit deals

items.question: How long does a Dodge engine replacement take?
items.answer: For common models like Caliber, Nitro or Journey, a like‑for‑like swap typically takes 3–5 working days. More complex vehicles like Ram 1500 or Charger/Challenger with HEMI V8s may require 5–7 days due to additional ancillary removal. The biggest variable is engine availability—our network stocks commonly replaced codes (ED3, ENR, ERB, EZH) to minimise downtime.
items.cta: Use our free comparison tool to check specialist lead times

items.question: Do reconditioned Dodge engines come with warranty?
items.answer: Yes, every reconditioned Dodge engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty covering major internals: crankshaft, pistons, timing components and oil pump. Extended 24‑month coverage is often available on premium rebuilt and remanufactured units. Always confirm exact terms directly with your chosen supplier, as coverage for ancillaries (turbo, injectors) can vary. Warranty protection is a key advantage over used engines.
items.cta: Find reconditioned Dodge engines with comprehensive warranty cover

items.question: Should I choose a used or reconditioned Dodge engine?
items.answer: Used Dodge engines (£800–£2,500) are cheaper upfront and suitable for temporary fixes or vehicles you plan to sell soon. Reconditioned units (£2,200–£5,800) cost more but include new components, upgraded parts addressing known failures (e.g., Pentastar head revisions), and a 12‑month warranty—typically delivering another 100k+ miles of reliable service. For failure‑prone engines like the 2.8L CRD or 5.7L HEMI, reconditioned with upgrades is strongly recommended.
items.cta: Use our free comparison tool to see whether a rebuilt or used Dodge engine makes financial sense

items.question: Where can I find Dodge engine specialists near me?
items.answer: EngineMarket connects you with a UK‑wide network of vetted Dodge specialists, with strong coverage in London, Manchester, the Midlands and South East. Our registration lookup matches you with nearby suppliers who stock your specific engine code (ED3, ENR, ERB, EZH) and offer fitting services. Nationwide delivery is available if no local specialist has your unit in stock—simply enter your reg to see live availability.
items.cta: Get quotes from UK Dodge engine specialists

items.question: What are the most common Dodge engine failures?
items.answer: The most frequent catastrophic failures driving replacement are: 2.8L CRD turbo vane sticking and injector wear (Nitro/Journey); 3.7L PowerTech timing chain guide fragmentation (Nitro/Dakota); pre‑2016 3.6L Pentastar cylinder head porosity (Ram/Charger); 5.7L HEMI MDS lifter collapse scoring camshafts; and 3.0L EcoDiesel oil cooler gasket failure causing bearing wear. Early detection rarely prevents these from becoming catastrophic, making full replacement the most common outcome for affected engines.
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Dodge engines with upgraded failure‑point components

items.question: How can I get the best price on a Dodge engine?
items.answer: Compare multiple quotes—prices for the same engine code (e.g., ERB Pentastar) can vary by £1,000+ across suppliers. Consider used engines for tight budgets or short‑term ownership, but opt for reconditioned units with upgraded components if you plan to keep your Dodge long‑term. Ask whether fitting, delivery and VAT are included, and check if supply‑only is an option if you have a trusted local garage. Small details significantly impact the final cost.
items.cta: Compare Dodge engine replacement prices now
